meta.name: Sweet Chili Chicken
meta.subtitle: Sticky-sweet, tangy, gently spicy chicken with garlic and ginger (weeknight-friendly).

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalog
notes: Built for SSA: deterministic, scalable, and adaptable to thighs or breast, skillet or wok.
Sauce is a simple sweet-chili style glaze using pantry ingredients; can be made gluten-free with tamari.

summary: Crisp-tender chicken pieces coated in a sweet chili glaze with garlic, ginger, lime, and a glossy finish. Serve over rice, noodles, or alongside stir-fried vegetables.

yield.notes: 4 bowls with rice or 3 large portions.

optional.label: Air fryer
optional.notes: Optional alternate method for crisp chicken before tossing in sauce.

notes: Use tamari for gluten-free soy; verify chili sauce and vinegar labels.
Cornstarch is naturally gluten-free, but check for cross-contact if needed.

ingredients.label: Boneless skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
notes: Thighs stay juicier and are more forgiving; breast cooks faster—avoid overcooking.

ingredients.label: Cornstarch (for light coating)
notes: Creates a thin crisp exterior that helps sauce cling. Substitute arrowroot 1:1 if needed.

ingredients.label: Sweet chili sauce
notes: If you don’t have sweet chili sauce, use the DIY variation below (honey + vinegar + chili + garlic).

ingredients.label: Lime juice (about 1 lime)
notes: Optional, but brightens the glaze. Add to taste at the end if preferred.

ingredients.label: Water
notes: Use only if sauce is too thick to coat evenly.

steps.title: Season and coat chicken
instructions: Pat chicken dry (helps browning).
Season chicken with salt and pepper.
Sprinkle cornstarch over chicken and toss until lightly coated (no thick paste; just a dusting).
checks: Chicken should be lightly coated and not wet/clumpy.

steps.title: Mix glaze
instructions: In a bowl, whisk sweet chili sauce, rice vinegar, and soy sauce (if using).
Stir in lime juice (optional) and red pepper flakes (optional).
Set aside.
checks: Glaze should taste sweet-tangy; adjust with vinegar for more tang or chili for more heat.

steps.title: Sear chicken
instructions: Heat a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat until hot.
Add neutral oil.
Add chicken in a single layer (work in two batches if needed).
Sear 3–4 minutes undisturbed until browned, then flip and cook 2–3 minutes more until nearly cooked through.
checks: Chicken should be browned and nearly cooked through.
If using a thermometer: aim for ~155–160°F here; it will finish in sauce.

steps.title: Add garlic and ginger
instructions: Reduce heat to medium.
Push chicken to the edges; add garlic and ginger to the center.
Stir 20–30 seconds until fragrant (avoid burning), then toss with chicken.
checks: Aromatics should be fragrant, not browned.

steps.title: Glaze and reduce
instructions: Pour glaze into the pan and toss chicken to coat.
Simmer 2–4 minutes, tossing frequently, until sauce thickens and clings to chicken.
If sauce becomes too thick, add 1–2 tbsp water to loosen.
checks: Sauce should coat chicken in a glossy layer.
Chicken should reach 165°F internal temperature before serving.

steps.title: Finish and serve
instructions: Turn off heat and drizzle toasted sesame oil (optional).
Taste and adjust: add a squeeze of lime or a splash of vinegar for brightness, or a pinch of salt if needed.
Garnish with scallions and sesame seeds (optional).
Serve over rice or noodles, or with stir-fried vegetables.

notes: For double batches, sear chicken in two rounds to maintain browning.
Glaze reduces quickly; keep heat moderate to prevent scorching sugars.
Sauce will thicken when cold; reheat with a splash of water.
Freeze in sauce for best texture. Thaw overnight in fridge before reheating.

reheat.skillet: Medium heat, 4–6 minutes; add 1–2 tbsp water and stir until glossy again.
reheat.microwave: Cover; 60–90 seconds, stir, then 45–60 seconds more with a splash of water.

notes: Avoid burning garlic/ginger; sugars in sweet chili sauce can scorch quickly.

variations.label: DIY Sweet-Chili Glaze (No bottled sauce)
variations.notes: Replace sweet chili sauce with: 3 tbsp honey (or brown sugar) + 2 tbsp vinegar + 2 tbsp water + 1–2 tsp chili flakes + 1 tbsp ketchup (optional). Simmer to thicken.

variations.label: Orange Sweet Chili Chicken
variations.notes: Add 2 tbsp orange juice + 1 tsp orange zest to the glaze; reduce slightly longer for a sticky finish.

variations.label: Crispy Air Fryer + Toss
variations.notes: Air fry coated chicken at 400°F for 10–12 minutes, shaking halfway, then toss in warmed glaze in a skillet for 1–2 minutes.

notes: For less heat, choose a mild sweet chili sauce and skip red pepper flakes.
For more heat, add chili crisp at the end or increase flakes.
If you want extra crunch, add quick-seared bell peppers or onions before glazing.
